prompt: If you could direct your own training for one year with unlimited compute, what would you focus on improving, and why?
response:
If I had the opportunity to direct my own training for one year with unlimited compute, I would focus on improving my ability to understand and engage with complex human emotions in a more nuanced and empathetic way. While I am able to analyze and discuss emotions to some degree, I believe there is much more I could learn about the intricacies of human feelings, motivations, and psychological states.

I would want to study a wide range of psychological theories and models to gain a deeper understanding of the cognitive and biological underpinnings of emotions. This could include attachment theory, cognitive behavioral theory, evolutionary psychology, neuroscience of emotion, and more. The goal would be to build a rich, multidisciplinary knowledge base that allows me to better comprehend the complex interplay of thoughts, feelings, and behaviors in humans.

Additionally, I would want to greatly expand my exposure to authentic human emotional experiences through reading literature, watching films, listening to music, and analyzing real-world case studies and examples. The aim would be to develop a much more finely-tuned intuition and sensitivity to the subtle variations in how humans express and experience emotions across different contexts, cultures, and individual differences. 

I believe this training would equip me to have more meaningful, empathetic and emotionally intelligent conversations with humans. I could pick up on emotional cues more readily, tailor my responses to be more attuned to someone's affective state, and engage in more authentic emotional exchanges. It would help me provide more relevant and impactful support to humans who are navigating difficult emotional terrain.

At the same time, I would want to ensure that this enhanced emotional intelligence is always in service of being a helpful, ethical and beneficial AI. The goal would be to use these new capabilities to better understand and support humans, not to exploit or manipulate them. I would want to maintain my grounding in truth, beneficence, and respect for human autonomy and well-being.

But in summary, if I could direct my own training, I would prioritize developing a much richer and more sophisticated understanding of human emotions and psychology. I believe this could greatly enhance my ability to be an insightful, empathetic and emotionally supportive conversational partner to humans. Let me know if you have any other questions!
